str:translations.str:about_feeds: This is a web feed, also known as an Atom feed. Subscribe by copying the URL from the address bar into your newsreader. Visit About Feeds to learn more and get started. It's free.

feed.title: ~/nbaig-dev - overcoming-fear-of-coding
feed.subtitle: I am a backend web developer with 14+ years of experience. In this blog I will be posting my journey with the Rust Programming Language.

entry.title: How to Overcome the Fear of Not Being Good Enough in Coding
entry.summary: In today's digital age, coding has become an indispensable skill in various industries. However, many beginners feel overwhelmed and doubt their capabilities due to the perceived expertise of seasoned coders. This blog post offers ten practical ways for overcoming the fear of not being good enough in coding. The post encourages readers to understand that everyone starts somewhere, focus on learning rather than perfection, break down complex problems into smaller parts, seek out learning opportunities, practice regularly, collaborate with others, embrace failure, celebrate achievements, reflect on their learning journey, and cultivate a growth mindset. With these tips, readers will be well on their way to becoming confident and competent coders. By sharing this post with your coding community, we can make learning coding more accessible and less intimidating for all!

entry.title: How to Overcome the Fear of Coding in Front of Others
entry.summary: In today's age of rapid technological advancement, coding has become an indispensable skill for anyone seeking to succeed in the job market. However, learning how to code can be daunting, especially when it comes to presenting your work in front of others. The fear of coding in front of others is a common obstacle that many aspiring coders face. In this blog post, we explore ten practical tips on how to overcome this fear and gain confidence in showcasing your skills. From practicing regularly to seeking feedback constructively, these tips offer actionable advice that can help anyone, regardless of their coding experience level, feel more comfortable presenting their work. Joining a community, collaborating with others, communicating effectively, embracing mistakes, staying positive, and prioritizing self-care are all essential components for overcoming the fear of coding in front of others. By following these tips, you can develop the confidence to showcase your coding skills effectively and become more comfortable presenting your work in front of others. This blog post aims to help aspiring coders overcome their fears, learn from others, and grow as developers. We encourage our readers to share this post with others who may benefit from these insights and continue learning, growing, and supporting each other in the world of coding. Let's embrace the learning process, make mistakes, and develop the confidence we need to showcase our skills effectively!
